cards.prompt: There are seven days in a week.
cards.explanation: A week is made up of seven days.

cards.prompt: A triangle has three sides.
cards.explanation: By definition, a triangle is a shape with three sides.

cards.prompt: An adult human has about 206 bones.
cards.explanation: An adult human skeleton has about 206 bones.

cards.prompt: Honey is made by bees.
cards.explanation: Bees make honey from the nectar of flowers.

cards.prompt: The largest animal on Earth is the blue whale.
cards.explanation: The blue whale is the largest animal known to have lived on Earth.

cards.prompt: There are twelve months in a year.
cards.explanation: A calendar year is divided into twelve months.

cards.prompt: A square has five sides.
cards.explanation: A square has four equal sides, not five.

cards.prompt: Mixing blue and yellow paint makes red.
cards.explanation: Mixing blue and yellow paint makes green.

cards.prompt: Spiders have six legs.
cards.explanation: Spiders have eight legs; insects have six.

cards.prompt: There are 24 hours in a week.
cards.explanation: There are 24 hours in a day; a week has 168 hours.

cards.prompt: The freezing point of water is 100 degrees Celsius.
cards.explanation: Water freezes at 0 degrees Celsius; it boils at about 100 degrees Celsius.

cards.prompt: An adult human normally has three eyes.
cards.explanation: A human normally has two eyes.

cards.prompt: The Earth revolves around the Sun.
cards.explanation: The Earth orbits the Sun, completing one full revolution in about 365 days.

cards.prompt: Goldfish can only remember things for three seconds.
cards.explanation: This is a myth; studies show goldfish can remember things for weeks or even months.

cards.prompt: Penguins are birds.
cards.explanation: Penguins are flightless birds; they have feathers and lay eggs like other birds.

cards.prompt: The Great Wall of China is easily visible from the Moon with the naked eye.
cards.explanation: This is a popular myth; the wall is far too narrow to be seen from the Moon without aid.

cards.prompt: There are 60 minutes in an hour.
cards.explanation: An hour is divided into 60 minutes, and each minute into 60 seconds.

cards.prompt: A tomato is botanically classified as a vegetable.
cards.explanation: Botanically, a tomato is a fruit because it develops from a flower and contains seeds.

cards.prompt: Paris is the capital of France.
cards.explanation: Paris is the capital and largest city of France.

cards.prompt: Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
cards.explanation: Lightning often strikes the same place repeatedly; tall buildings like the Empire State Building are hit many times a year.

cards.prompt: Water is made of hydrogen and oxygen.
cards.explanation: A water molecule (H2O) is made of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.

cards.prompt: Ostriches bury their heads in the sand when they are scared.
cards.explanation: This is a myth; ostriches may lie low or run from danger, but they do not bury their heads.

cards.prompt: A leap year has 366 days.
cards.explanation: In a leap year an extra day is added to February, giving the year 366 days instead of 365.

cards.prompt: Bulls charge at capes because the color red makes them angry.
cards.explanation: Cattle cannot distinguish red from green; it is the movement of the cape, not its color, that provokes the bull.

cards.prompt: Bats are mammals.
cards.explanation: Bats are the only mammals capable of true sustained flight; they give birth to live young and nurse them with milk.

cards.prompt: A dozen means twenty items.
cards.explanation: A dozen means twelve items; twenty items is sometimes called a score.

cards.prompt: The Pacific is the largest ocean on Earth.
cards.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest ocean, covering about a third of the Earth's surface.

cards.prompt: Bananas grow on trees.
cards.explanation: Banana plants are actually giant herbaceous plants, not true trees; their 'trunk' is made of tightly packed leaf bases.

cards.prompt: A carrot is a root vegetable.
cards.explanation: The part of the carrot we eat is the plant's taproot, which grows underground.

cards.prompt: Humans use only 10 percent of their brains.
cards.explanation: This is a myth; brain scans show that virtually all parts of the brain are active over the course of a day.

cards.prompt: A century is a period of 100 years.
cards.explanation: A century means 100 years, just as a decade means 10 years.

cards.prompt: An octagon has six sides.
cards.explanation: An octagon has eight sides; a six-sided shape is called a hexagon.

cards.prompt: An octopus has eight arms.
cards.explanation: Octopuses have eight flexible arms lined with suckers.

cards.prompt: The Statue of Liberty is located in London.
cards.explanation: The Statue of Liberty stands in New York Harbor in the United States; it was a gift from France.

cards.prompt: The human heart pumps blood around the body.
cards.explanation: The heart is a muscular pump that circulates blood, carrying oxygen and nutrients throughout the body.

cards.prompt: Antarctica is the hottest continent on Earth.
cards.explanation: Antarctica is the coldest continent; the hottest average temperatures are found in Africa.
